title: WF-011 Sensitive Export Approval to Download — maker-checker + private generation + secure download
business_reason: Third chain under the workflow-at-a-time rule (WF-011) — and the highest-risk: a staff member exports sensitive data only after two-person approval, the file is watermarked + stored privately, and downloaded via a short-lived signed URL that expires. The scaffold had the request lifecycle + dual-approval transition + a watermark exporter, but the request create demanded a client requested_by, the generate transition produced no file, and there was no secure download.

design.request: POST /api/staff/reports/exports — reason required (high-risk); requested_by is SERVER-set (the actor, never client — a custom route because requested_by is a staff FK and the broad generator fix would break school-scoped *_by columns); default active report_definition resolved server-side; status submitted. GET lists requests.
design.approval: the generated export-requests approve transition is dualApproval (maker-checker): recordApproval needs TWO DISTINCT approvers; one actor can never self-approve to applied. Reuses the kernel — no new approval code.
design.generate: POST /api/staff/reports/exports/[id]/generate — only when approved; runs toCsv (watermarked + classified) -> report_snapshot + storeFile to a PRIVATE bucket -> export_files (file_ref, expires_at, watermark) -> status generated.
design.download: GET /api/staff/reports/exports/[id]/download — expiry-gated (expired -> 409 + download_status=expired); short-lived signed URL (never the path, never logged); export_download_events + audit.
design.ui: custom /staff/reports/exports page (request form + list + generate/download actions), nav-linked via a new gen_ui STAFF_CUSTOM_NAV mechanism (link only, page not generated).

param_14.question: How is a sensitive data export controlled end-to-end?
param_14.answer: A staff member requests it with a reason (requested_by server-set). It must be approved by TWO distinct staff (maker-checker — one person can never self-approve). Only then can it be generated: a watermarked, classified file is produced and stored privately with an expiry. It is downloaded via a short-lived signed URL (never the storage path), every download is audited, and an expired export is blocked.
param_14.answer_source: reports_exports lifecycle (dualApproval approve), exporter.ts (watermark), export_files.expires_at, WF-011
param_14.security_implications: maker-checker (2 distinct approvers; self-approval cannot apply) + reason on every step; requested_by server-set (never client); private storage + short-lived signed URL (path never returned/logged); watermark + classification on the file; expiry enforced; every download audited (granted/denied/expired).
param_14.approval_implications: generation is blocked until two-person approval; the whole flow is staff-only.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: the per-report DATA query (rows are empty now — the secured pipeline is the deliverable); xlsx/pdf formats; export expiry sweep worker.
param_14.assumptions: an active report_definition exists; staff actors are staff_profiles (requested_by FK).
